commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=9094aa79bf260bfec4bbbeb6f67e3682d627f24a)
commands.review_summary:
The branch emits explicit close and close-required markers for PR close reviews, maps trusted close markers to autoclose, adds PR head-SHA gating, and covers the rendering/router behavior with focused tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main renders PR close decisions as needs-human and the trusted parser has no close-marker branch, so a close-intent review cannot reach autoclose on current main.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human handling is appropriate because maintainers need to decide whether trusted close comments may bypass the durable apply close gates or must be routed through them before mutation.

target.title: [codex] Route PR close reviews to autoclose
target.body:
## Summary

- Emit an explicit `clawsweeper-verdict:close` and `clawsweeper-action:close-required` marker for pull request reports whose structured decision is `close`.
- Teach the trusted comment router to map those close markers into the existing autoclose path so a ClawSweeper close-intent review can close the PR instead of pausing as `needs-human`.
- Gate trusted autoclose markers against the reviewed PR head SHA before executing the close.

## Root cause

PR close reviews rendered visible close language such as `So I'm closing this here`, but the hidden automation marker was still `clawsweeper-verdict:needs-human`. That made the public comment and the automation contract disagree: maintainers saw a close recommendation, while the router treated the same comment as a human-review pause.

## Back-sweep list

The 2026-06-25 scan found 138 currently-open OpenClaw PRs with ClawSweeper duplicate/superseded close-language comments. These are the items to close in a follow-up back sweep after this lands, with live state rechecked immediately before mutation.

Scan candidate count: 436; verified matching open PRs: 138.
Buckets: 75 close recommendation left open; 63 contradictory keep-open/closing comment.

| PR | Last matching comment | Bucket |
|---:|---|---|
| [#96781](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96781) | 2026-06-25T12:48:03Z | close recommendation left open |
| [#96761](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96761) | 2026-06-25T11:45:56Z | close recommendation left open |
| [#96759](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96759) | 2026-06-25T11:40:39Z | close recommendation left open |

## Real Behavior Proof

Captured locally on 2026-06-25 against commit `9094aa79bf260bfec4bbbeb6f67e3682d627f24a` after `pnpm run build:all`.

The proof script imports the built `dist` modules, renders a PR close report, parses the rendered trusted ClawSweeper comment, and checks the same stale-head helper used by the autoclose classifier.

```text
$ node - <close-marker-proof-script>
��close marker lines:
<!-- clawsweeper-verdict:close item=96097 sha=reviewed-close-sha confidence=high reason=duplicate_or_superseded -->
<!-- clawsweeper-action:close-required item=96097 sha=reviewed-close-sha confidence=high reason=duplicate_or_superseded -->

trusted automation parse:
{
  "intent": "autoclose",
  "trusted_bot": true,
  "expected_head_sha": "reviewed-close-sha",
  "close_reason": "duplicate_or_superseded",
  "autoclose_message": "structured ClawSweeper close marker: close-required (sha=reviewed-close-sha)"
}

stale-head guard:
{
  "staleBlock": "ClawSweeper close marker targets a stale PR head SHA",
  "freshBlock": null
}

```

This shows:

- PR close reports now render `clawsweeper-verdict:close` and `clawsweeper-action:close-required` with the reviewed head SHA and close reason.
- A trusted ClawSweeper close marker parses to `intent: "autoclose"` rather than `needs-human`.
- The stale-head guard blocks when the live PR head differs and returns `null` when it matches.
- The existing autoclose executor re-fetches each target before mutation with `fetchIssue(target.number)`, so the follow-up back sweep must still recheck live state before closing each PR.

## Validation Output

```text
$ pnpm run build:all

```

```text
$ pnpm exec node --test test/review-comment-rendering.test.ts test/repair/comment-router-core.test.ts

```

```text
$ pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/clawsweeper.ts src/repair/comment-router-core.ts src/repair/comment-router.ts test/review-comment-rendering.test.ts test/repair/comment-router-core.test.ts
��Checking formatting...

All matched files use the correct format.
Finished in 87ms on 5 files using 8 threads.

```

## Validation

The exact command outputs are included above. Summary:

- `pnpm run build:all`
- `pnpm exec node --test test/review-comment-rendering.test.ts test/repair/comment-router-core.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/clawsweeper.ts src/repair/comment-router-core.ts src/repair/comment-router.ts test/review-comment-rendering.test.ts test/repair/comment-router-core.test.ts`

## Notes

The back-sweep list intentionally includes the full 138-item scan requested by maintainers. The close executor should still re-fetch each item before closing so already-closed PRs, changed heads, or new human follow-up are skipped according to current gates.

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
